channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ethiopian man’s South African driver’s licence under investigation after alleged drunk driving arrest
item.description: The Road Traffic Inspectorate (RTI) has launched an investigation following the arrest of an Ethiopian man in Port Shepstone, KwaZulu-Natal, who was allegedly driving under the influence of alcohol. T...

item.title: County Officer Urges Pedestrians to Use Footbridges for Safety Along Busy Roads
item.description: Nairobi - Chief Officer Geoffrey Mosiria of Customer Care Services has expressed concern over the increasing trend of pedestrians ignoring footbridges along busy roads, particularly Mombasa Road. Mosi...

item.title: DILG enforces ban on public street drinking of alcohol
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has reiterated that drinking alcohol in public streets is strictly prohibited across the Philippines. This directive is part of the agency's ...

item.title: Changes to Universal Credit health top-up reduce payments for new claimants
item.description: From 6 April, new applicants for the health element of Universal Credit will receive half the payment given to existing claimants, reducing it from £429.80 to £217.26 per month. This change is intende...

item.title: Eight new youth hubs in England to support young people with employment and crime prevention
item.description: The UK government has announced the opening of eight Young Futures youth hubs across England, aimed at supporting young people aged up to 18 in employment, health, and wellbeing, while steering them a...

item.title: James Rodríguez hospitalized due to severe dehydration, condition stabilizing
item.description: James Rodríguez, former Real Madrid and Bayern Munich midfielder, was recently hospitalized in the United States due to severe dehydration after returning from international duty with Colombia. Minnes...

item.title: Dian Forrester impresses on PSL debut despite Rawalpindiz defeat
item.description: South African cricket continues to produce exciting young talent, with 25-year-old Dian Forrester making a strong impression on his Pakistan Super League debut. Forrester, who recently made his T20I d...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts April Rainfall Across Counties
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has released its monthly weather outlook for April 2026, predicting varied rainfall patterns across the country. Communities are being advised to prepare for ...

item.title: Expert Analysis on the Regional Impacts of the Iran Conflict
item.description: Following the February 28 offensive by the US and Israel against Iran, the Middle East has entered a period of significant instability. The conflict escalated rapidly, with Iran retaliating against US...

item.title: Trump Extends Iran Deadline by 24 Hours Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: On Sunday, April 5, 2026, U.S. President Donald Trump issued a brief statement on his social media platform, extending by 24 hours the deadline for Iran to reach a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz....

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Opposes Three-Language Policy, Criticises Centre on Hindi Imposition
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin has firmly opposed the three-language formula under the National Education Policy, stating that it amounts to an imposition of Hindi on the state’s people. Speaking...

item.title: Contraband confiscated during surprise raid at Pollsmoor Correctional Centre
item.description: During a surprise raid at the Pollsmoor Correctional Centre in Cape Town on Saturday evening, prison officials confiscated a range of contraband including drugs, cellphones, cash, routers, digital acc...

item.title: UK MPs criticised for attending promotional events of Dubai property linked to Malik Riaz
item.description: Three British parliamentarians of Pakistani origin, including Labour MPs Naz Shah and Afzal Khan, along with independent Ayoub Khan, came under scrutiny for appearing at promotional events for a Dubai...

item.title: Fuel prices fall sharply after tax cuts and Cyclone Maila monitored near Queensland
item.description: This week in Australia, petrol prices across all major cities have dropped significantly, with reductions of around 31 to 35 cents per litre following federal and state government fuel tax cuts. The f...

item.title: GSIS to Refund Over P19 Billion to Members Under Balik Ginhawa Program
item.description: The Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) announced it will refund more than P19 billion in loan payments to approximately 1.37 million members and pensioners through its Balik Ginhawa program. U...

item.title: TFF Confirms Damaro’s Eligibility, Dismissing Simba’s Protest
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Tanzania Football Federation (TFF) has officially dismissed Simba SC’s protest regarding the eligibility of Young Africans SC’s midfielder, Mohammed Damaro Camara. Simba had argued...

item.title: Commission of Inquiry set to release findings on 2025 election violence
item.description: The Commission of Inquiry investigating the violence surrounding Tanzania's October 2025 General Election is about to submit its findings. President Samia Suluhu Hassan formed the nine-member commissi...

item.title: Canadian Passport Applications to Receive Refund if Processing Exceeds 30 Days
item.description: Starting April 1, 2026, Canadian passport applicants are eligible for a full refund if their application takes longer than 30 business days to process. The federal government, through Immigration, Ref...

item.title: IMF urges Pakistan to address fuel price distortions despite subsidy measures
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has advised Pakistan to remove distortions in petroleum pricing as soon as possible, despite acknowledging the Rs152 billion subsidy extended by the federal gover...

item.title: UK Government Details Near-Activations of Emergency Alert System
item.description: The UK government has disclosed several incidents that nearly warranted the activation of the national emergency alert system, which sends messages and sirens to mobile phones to warn of life-threaten...
